heroTitle: The oldest form of botanical medicine there is
heroDesc: Guided walks in nature with a clinical herbalist. Identify plants, discover their medicinal uses, and reconnect with the seasonality that our ancestors knew by heart.
openingTitle: Nature is not a backdrop — it is a pharmacy
openingP1: For millennia, human beings learned to know the plants around them. They knew what relieved fever, what calmed anxiety, what accelerated the healing of a wound. This knowledge was passed from generation to generation.
openingP2: We lost it in a few decades. These walks are a way to find it again — one plant at a time, one season at a time, in the green spaces we pass through without seeing them.
forestBathingTitle: Not a hike. Not a lecture. Something different.
forestBathingDesc1: The Japanese call it shinrin-yoku. Forest bathing. The measurable, documented reduction in cortisol that happens when you spend time moving slowly through green space, paying attention.
forestBathingDesc2: These walks are not nature appreciation classes. They are a guided experience of the landscape as a living pharmacy — combined with the well-evidenced benefits of slow, attentive movement outdoors.

seasons.name: 🌱 Spring
seasons.desc: The renewal of cleansing and toning plants. Nettle, dandelion, elderflower, ground ivy. The moment for liver and lymphatic support after winter.

seasons.name: ☀️ Summer
seasons.desc: The peak of medicinal abundance. St John's wort in full flower, yarrow, lemon balm, lavender. Plants of joy, digestion and warmth.
seasons.plants: St John's Wort · Yarrow · Lemon balm · Lavender

seasons.name: 🍂 Autumn
seasons.desc: The harvest and preparation for winter. Hawthorn berries, rosehip, ginkgo, adaptogenic mushrooms. Immune strengthening and nervous system support.

seasons.name: ❄️ Winter
seasons.desc: The wisdom of winter plants. Thyme, elderberry, rosemary, pine. Respiratory support, warmth and vitality in the calm of dormancy.

expectItems.title: Plant identification
expectItems.desc: Learn to recognise medicinal plants in their natural habitat — their forms, textures, smells, the characteristics that allow safe identification.

expectItems.title: Medicinal uses
expectItems.desc: For each plant encountered, we explore its history of use, recognised medicinal properties, and how it can be simply integrated into daily life.

expectItems.title: Ethical foraging
expectItems.desc: How to forage respectfully — legal rules, environmental ethics, drying and preservation methods. Foraging as conscious practice.

expectItems.title: Shinrin-yoku
expectItems.desc: The Japanese forest bath — documented benefits of nature immersion on cortisol, immunity and mental wellbeing. Walk slowly, breathe deeply, observe.

expectItems.title: Small group, real exchange
expectItems.desc: Maximum 12 participants to allow individual questions, genuine transmission and an atmosphere of shared curiosity rather than lecture.

expectItems.title: In English or French
expectItems.desc: Walks can be organised entirely in English, French, or bilingually depending on participants. Mention your preference when booking.

mailingDesc: Walk dates are released to the mailing list before anywhere else. Given the small group size, they fill quickly.
